act.long_title: An Act to define and amend the law relating to sale of goods
act.note: Carved out from Contract Act 1872 (ss. 76–123 repealed in ICA). SOGA 1930 is therefore a lex specialis — Contract Act applies for everything not specifically covered here.

parts.title: Chapter II — Formation of the Contract
Chapter III — Effects of the Contract
Chapter IV — Performance of the Contract
Chapter V — Rights of Unpaid Seller Against the Goods
Chapter VI — Suits for Breach of the Contract

nodes.title: Short title and commencement
nodes.summary: Act may be called the Sale of Goods Act, 1930. Extends to whole of India.

nodes.title: Definitions
nodes.summary: Defines: 'buyer', 'seller', 'goods', 'future goods', 'specific goods', 'existing goods', 'document of title to goods', 'delivery', 'insolvent', 'mercantile agent', 'property', 'quality of goods'.

cross_links.reason: General definitions in Contract Act apply where SOGA does not define a term

nodes.title: Sale and agreement to sell
nodes.summary: Contract of sale = seller transfers or agrees to transfer property in goods to buyer for a price. Where property transferred immediately = sale; where future or subject to conditions = agreement to sell.

cross_links.reason: Contract of sale must satisfy requirements of valid contract under Contract Act §10

nodes.title: How contract of sale is made
nodes.summary: Contract of sale may be: (a) absolute or conditional; (b) oral or written or partly both; (c) express or implied. No form required.

cross_links.reason: Express and implied promises — formation principles from Contract Act apply

nodes.title: Existing or future goods
nodes.summary: Goods that are in existence at time of contract = existing goods. Goods to be manufactured or acquired after = future goods. Contract for future goods = agreement to sell only, not sale.

nodes.title: Goods perishing before making of contract
nodes.summary: Where parties contract for sale of specific goods but goods have perished without seller's knowledge, contract is void.

cross_links.reason: Void contract on mutual mistake as to existence of subject matter — same principle in Contract Act §20

nodes.title: Goods perishing before sale but after agreement to sell
nodes.summary: Where agreement to sell specific goods and goods subsequently perish without fault of either party, agreement is avoided (frustrated).

cross_links.reason: Frustration of contract under §56 Contract Act — §8 SOGA is a specific application of the same doctrine for goods

nodes.title: Ascertainment of price
nodes.summary: Price may be: (a) fixed by contract; (b) left to be fixed by third party valuer; (c) determined by course of dealing between parties.

nodes.title: Agreement to sell at valuation
nodes.summary: Where price to be fixed by third party and that party fails to fix price, agreement is avoided. If goods delivered and buyer retains them, he must pay a reasonable price.

nodes.title: Stipulations as to time
nodes.summary: Stipulations as to time of payment are not conditions of contract of sale unless parties so intend. Stipulations as to time of delivery — whether condition depends on terms of contract.

cross_links.reason: Time as essence of contract — §55 Contract Act; §11 SOGA creates special rule for payment timings in sale

nodes.title: Condition and warranty
nodes.summary: Condition = stipulation essential to the main purpose of contract, breach entitles repudiation. Warranty = collateral stipulation, breach gives right to damages only, not repudiation.

cross_links.reason: Repudiation of contract on breach of condition — right to treat contract as discharged per §39 Contract Act

nodes.title: When condition to be treated as warranty
nodes.summary: Buyer may treat breach of condition as breach of warranty when: (a) waived; (b) treated as warranty; (c) where contract not severable and buyer has accepted goods or part of them.

nodes.title: Implied undertakings as to title etc.
nodes.summary: In every contract of sale: (a) implied condition seller has right to sell; (b) implied warranty buyer will have quiet possession; (c) implied warranty goods are free from encumbrance.

nodes.title: Sale by description
nodes.summary: Where there is a sale by description, implied condition that goods shall correspond with description. If sale is by sample and description — goods must correspond with both.

nodes.title: Implied conditions as to quality or fitness
nodes.summary: No implied condition as to quality or fitness for any particular purpose EXCEPT: (a) buyer discloses purpose and relies on seller's skill/judgment; (b) merchant seller — goods must be of merchantable quality. Proviso: defects reasonably discoverable on examination.

cross_links.reason: Active concealment of defect = fraud under §17 Contract Act — seller may be liable beyond §16 SOGA

nodes.title: Sale by sample
nodes.summary: In sale by sample: (a) bulk must correspond with sample; (b) buyer must have opportunity to compare; (c) goods must be free from defect rendering them unmerchantable not apparent on reasonable examination.

nodes.title: Goods must be ascertained
nodes.summary: Where there is a contract for unascertained goods, no property in goods passes to buyer until goods are ascertained.

nodes.title: Property passes when intended to pass
nodes.summary: Property in specific or ascertained goods passes to buyer at time parties intend it to pass. Intent inferred from terms, conduct, circumstances.

nodes.title: Specific goods in a deliverable state
nodes.summary: In unconditional contract for specific goods in deliverable state, property passes when contract made, even if payment or delivery deferred.

nodes.title: Specific goods to be put in deliverable state
nodes.summary: Where seller must do something to specific goods to put them in deliverable state, property doesn't pass until that thing is done and buyer has notice.

nodes.title: Specific goods in deliverable state when seller has to do anything thereto
nodes.summary: Where goods are in deliverable state but seller has to weigh, measure or test them to ascertain the price, property does not pass until that act is done and buyer has notice.

nodes.title: Sale of unascertained goods and appropriation
nodes.summary: For unascertained or future goods sold by description, property passes when goods conforming to description are unconditionally appropriated to contract by either party with other's assent.

nodes.title: Goods sent on approval or 'on sale or return'
nodes.summary: Where goods delivered on approval or sale-or-return: property passes when buyer signifies approval, or acts adopting transaction, or retains goods beyond reasonable time.

nodes.title: Reservation of right of disposal
nodes.summary: Where seller reserves right of disposal until certain conditions fulfilled, property doesn't pass until conditions fulfilled even if goods delivered to buyer/carrier.

nodes.title: Risk prima facie passes with property
nodes.summary: Unless otherwise agreed, goods remain at seller's risk until property passes to buyer; after that, at buyer's risk — whether delivered or not.

nodes.title: Duties of seller and buyer
nodes.summary: Primary duty of seller is to deliver goods; primary duty of buyer is to accept and pay for goods in accordance with terms of contract.

cross_links.reason: Obligations of parties to perform their respective duties — general principles from Contract Act §37

nodes.title: Payment and delivery are concurrent conditions
nodes.summary: Payment and delivery are concurrent conditions — seller must be ready and willing to deliver in exchange for price, and buyer ready and willing to pay for delivery.

cross_links.reason: Concurrent conditions of performance — §51 Contract Act provides for simultaneous performance of reciprocal promises

nodes.title: Delivery
nodes.summary: Delivery = voluntary transfer of possession. May be actual, constructive or symbolic.

nodes.title: Effect of part delivery
nodes.summary: Delivery of part of goods in progress of delivery of whole = delivery of whole. Delivery of part as separate transaction does not operate as delivery of remainder.

nodes.title: Buyer to apply for delivery
nodes.summary: Apart from contract, seller not bound to deliver until buyer applies for it.

nodes.title: Rules as to delivery
nodes.summary: Place, time, and other rules for delivery: (1) at seller's place of business unless agreed otherwise; (2) seller to put goods in deliverable state; (3) demand or tender at reasonable hour; (4) expense of delivery on seller, of putting into deliverable state on buyer's request — by agreement.

nodes.title: Delivery of wrong quantity
nodes.summary: If seller delivers less — buyer may reject or accept what delivered at contract rate. If more — buyer may reject all, accept contracted quantity, or accept all at contract rate. If mixed — may reject or accept goods as contracted.

nodes.title: Instalment deliveries
nodes.summary: Buyer not bound to accept delivery in instalments unless agreed. Where instalment contract, breach of one instalment: depends on terms and circumstances whether whole contract is repudiated.

cross_links.reason: Anticipatory repudiation and breach of instalment — right to treat contract as discharged per Contract Act §39

nodes.title: Delivery to carrier or wharfinger
nodes.summary: Delivery to carrier for transmission to buyer = delivery to buyer. Seller must make reasonable contract with carrier; otherwise buyer may refuse to treat delivery to carrier as delivery to himself.

nodes.title: Risk where goods are delivered at distant place
nodes.summary: Where seller agrees to deliver goods at his own risk at a place other than where sold, buyer assumes risk of deterioration incidental to transit.

nodes.title: Buyer's right of examining goods
nodes.summary: Buyer not deemed to have accepted goods until reasonable opportunity to examine them for conformity with contract is given.

nodes.title: Acceptance
nodes.summary: Buyer is deemed to have accepted goods when: (a) intimates acceptance; (b) does act inconsistent with seller's ownership; (c) retains goods beyond reasonable time without rejection.

nodes.title: Buyer not bound to return rejected goods
nodes.summary: Buyer who has properly rejected goods is not bound to return them; it is sufficient to intimate that he refuses to accept.

nodes.title: Liability of buyer for neglecting or refusing delivery
nodes.summary: When seller ready and willing to deliver and buyer refuses to take delivery, buyer liable for reasonable charges for care and custody of goods.

nodes.title: Unpaid seller defined
nodes.summary: Seller is unpaid when: (a) whole price has not been paid or tendered; (b) bill of exchange or negotiable instrument given but has been dishonoured.

nodes.title: Unpaid seller's rights
nodes.summary: Unpaid seller has: (1) lien on goods while in possession; (2) right of stoppage in transit after parting with possession; (3) right of resale.

nodes.title: Seller's lien
nodes.summary: Unpaid seller in possession may retain goods until payment when: (a) no credit terms; (b) credit terms have expired; (c) buyer becomes insolvent.

nodes.title: Part delivery
nodes.summary: Where part of goods delivered, seller may exercise lien on remainder unless circumstances show intention to waive lien.

nodes.title: Termination of lien
nodes.summary: Seller's lien lost when: (a) goods delivered to carrier without reservation of right of disposal; (b) buyer/agent lawfully obtains possession; (c) waiver.

nodes.title: Right of stoppage in transit
nodes.summary: Where buyer becomes insolvent and goods are in transit, unpaid seller may resume possession of goods in transit and retain until payment.

nodes.title: Sale not generally rescinded by lien or stoppage in transit
nodes.summary: Exercise of lien or stoppage in transit does not rescind contract of sale; seller may still sue for price.

cross_links.reason: Seller's right to sue for price or damages — principles of Contract Act §73 apply

nodes.title: Seller's right of resale
nodes.summary: Unpaid seller may resell goods where: (a) goods are perishable; (b) seller gives notice of intention to resell and buyer does not pay within reasonable time; (c) seller expressly reserved right of resale.

nodes.title: Damages for non-delivery
nodes.summary: Where seller wrongfully neglects or refuses to deliver goods, buyer may sue for damages for non-delivery. Damages = estimated loss directly and naturally resulting from breach.

cross_links.reason: Damages principle = §73 Contract Act — loss directly and naturally resulting from breach at time of breach

nodes.title: Specific performance
nodes.summary: In any suit for breach of contract to deliver specific or ascertained goods, court may direct specific performance.

cross_links.reason: Specific performance under SOGA §58 is governed and enforced by SRA 1963 §10 onwards
SRA §14 lists contracts not specifically enforceable — goods with adequate money substitute may be excluded

nodes.title: Remedy for breach of warranty
nodes.summary: For breach of warranty buyer may: (a) set up breach in diminution of price; (b) sue for damages for breach of warranty. Buyer cannot reject goods solely on breach of warranty after acceptance.

cross_links.reason: Damages for breach of warranty quantified by Contract Act §73 principles

nodes.title: Repudiation of contract before due date
nodes.summary: Where either party repudiates contract before date of performance, other party may treat contract as subsisting and wait, or treat breach as already committed and sue.

cross_links.reason: Anticipatory breach (repudiation) — §39 Contract Act is the parent doctrine; §60 SOGA applies it to sale of goods

nodes.title: Interest by way of damages and special damages
nodes.summary: Court may award interest at such rate as it thinks fit. Special or remote damages may be awarded in circumstances contemplated by parties at time of contract.

cross_links.reason: Special damages only if in reasonable contemplation — mirrors remoteness rule in §73 Contract Act (Hadley v Baxendale test)

nodes.title: Exclusion of implied terms and conditions
nodes.summary: Implied terms, conditions and warranties may be negatived or varied by express agreement, or by course of dealing, or by usage of trade.

cross_links.reason: Exclusion of statutory implied terms cannot be used to achieve an unlawful object — §23 Contract Act

nodes.title: Reasonable time a question of fact
nodes.summary: Where reference is made to reasonable time in this Act, what is reasonable time is a question of fact.

nodes.title: Auction sales
nodes.summary: In sale by auction: (a) goods may be sold in lots; (b) completed by fall of hammer; (c) right to bid may be reserved; (d) seller or auctioneer may not bid in auction without disclosed reserve.

nodes.title: Liability of principal when agent exceeds authority
nodes.summary: Where agent is entrusted with goods and pledges same as security beyond authority, pledge valid only to extent of agent's authority.

cross_links.reason: Agent's authority — Contract Act §§182–238 govern; §65 SOGA applies in sale of goods context

nodes.title: Repeal of enactments
nodes.summary: Repeals sections 76–123 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 which previously governed sale of goods.

cross_links.reason: SOGA 1930 replaced ICA §§76-123 — this is the formal legislative severance provision
